reason: Geopolitical tensions involving Iran are escalating, leading to a significant spike in oil prices and broader market risk-off sentiment. Bitcoin is experiencing substantial ETF outflows ($9 billion over four months) and has broken key support levels, indicating strong selling pressure. While traditional financial institutions show continued long-term interest in digital assets, immediate macroeconomic and geopolitical headwinds are the dominant drivers, pointing to further short-term volatility and potential downside.
headlines: Bitcoin slips below $66,000, U.S. stock futures bleed as Iran hits Saudi oil refinery
Over $9 billion flees bitcoin and ether ETFs in four months
Bitcoin, cryptos under pressure as oil spikes 6% and global markets price in U.S.-Iran conflict
Hyperliquid's HYPE token jumps 5% as Iran war brings windfall revenue, JUP gains on supply freeze
Bitcoin losing trillions in value hasn't stopped traditional giants' interest in digital assets sector
